title: FCC approves first Reflect Orbital satellite
summary: The FCC has approved the first Reflect Orbital satellite, which aims to reflect sunlight into nighttime regions. The project has faced criticism from astronomers and environmentalists.

title: Earth observation satellites pass telecom in European space industry sales
summary: European space industry sales rebounded in 2025 after a contraction in 2024, with Earth observation satellites surpassing telecom in revenue. The growth was highlighted in Eurospace's latest Facts and Figures report.

title: China unveils members of state-backed commercial space consortium
summary: China's government published a membership list for a state-backed commercial space consortium, revealing key companies deemed established players by the state. This move provides insight into China's commercial space sector.

title: The space industry is weighing ambitious hiring against heritage
summary: The space industry is balancing the need for ambitious hiring with the value of experienced heritage workers. The challenge highlights the tension between innovation and traditional expertise in the sector.

title: Startup testing nuclear battery technology in orbit
summary: A City Labs mission is testing a tritium-powered nuclear battery in orbit to validate its effectiveness for satellites and lunar systems, particularly where solar power is insufficient.

title: Space capitalism needs more than a bull market
summary: Commercial space industry is thriving with increased activity in Washington, including executive orders and directives, but requires more than just a bull market for sustained growth. The focus on 'space superiority' highlights strategic priorities in the sector.

title: ElevationSpace advances work on commercial reentry vehicle
summary: Japanese startup ElevationSpace is progressing on its commercial reentry vehicle, securing customers and preparing for its first mission. The company is closely monitoring SpaceX's entry into the reentry vehicle market.

title: China becomes second country to recover orbital booster with Long March 10B
summary: China successfully launched its Long March 10B rocket and recovered the first stage, becoming the second country to achieve orbital booster recovery. This marks a significant advancement in China's reusable rocket technology.

title: Interior Department requests information on offshore launch options
summary: The U.S. Department of the Interior's office is soliciting ideas for offshore orbital launch concepts to alleviate congestion at traditional spaceports. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to expand launch capabilities beyond land-based facilities.

title: SDA TAP Lab Evolves Into BMC3I TAP Lab And In Partnership With Catalyst Campus Launches A New Multi-Phased Program To Accelerate Mission-Focused Technology Development
summary: The SDA TAP Lab has been rebranded as the BMC3I TAP Lab and, in partnership with Catalyst Campus, launched a multi-phased program to accelerate mission-focused technology development through collaboration between commercial innovators and government entities.

title: A ravenous black hole in our backyard could be our window into the ancient universe
summary: A nearby galaxy's feeding black hole mimics the behavior of ancient cosmic titans from the early universe, offering insights into the ancient cosmos. This discovery provides a unique window into the conditions of the early universe.

title: Launching from 2 continents: Germany's Isar Aerospace leases Canadian pad for $150 million
summary: German company Isar Aerospace has signed a $150 million agreement with Canada's Maritime Launch Services to lease a launch pad at Spaceport Nova Scotia, expanding its operations to Canada. This move marks Isar Aerospace's first overseas expansion.

title: SpaceX ignites all 33 powerful engines on Starship booster ahead of Flight 13 launch
summary: SpaceX successfully test-fired all 33 engines on the next Super Heavy booster for Starship's upcoming Flight 13, which may launch as soon as next week.

title: These rare glowing 'space clouds' are summer's best-kept skywatching secret
summary: Noctilucent clouds, rare glowing 'space clouds,' are best visible during summer and appear near the edge of space. These shimmering ice clouds are a skywatching phenomenon.

title: SpaceX launch creates colorful 'jellyfish' in the night sky | Space photo of the day for July 10, 2026
summary: A SpaceX launch created a visually striking 'jellyfish' effect in the night sky, captured in a space photo. The phenomenon was later confirmed to be unrelated to extraterrestrial activity.

title: The moon, Mars and the Pleiades form a stunning lineup before dawn on July 11. Here's how to see it
summary: A celestial lineup featuring the moon, Mars, Aldebaran, and star clusters Pleiades will be visible before dawn on July 11. Early risers can observe this stunning astronomical event.

title: Supreme Court ruling on mail-in ballots ensures astronauts can vote from space  — or anywhere else
summary: A U.S. Supreme Court ruling protecting mail-in ballots may extend voting rights to astronauts in space or training abroad. This ensures astronauts can participate in elections regardless of their location.

title: Astronomers may have heard the 1st 'whispers' of ghost particles created by supernova explosions
summary: Astronomers may have detected the first signals of neutrinos, often called 'ghost particles,' which are remnants of ancient supernova explosions. These particles could provide insights into the deaths of stars billions of years ago.

title: Don't miss out on 'The Ark' Season 3 — get a year-long Peacock TV deal for just over $9 a month
summary: Peacock TV is offering a year-long deal for just over $9 a month to watch 'The Ark' Season 3, a sci-fi series set in outer space. This promotion highlights the platform's growing content library and competitive pricing.

title: Making history! China lands rocket during an orbital launch for 1st time ever (video)
summary: China successfully completed its first-ever orbital launch with a rocket landing at sea using the Long March 10B. The debut launch on July 10 deployed a satellite into orbit.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20's 33 engines, marking a significant milestone in its development. The test advances SpaceX's progress toward future missions.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company's progress highlights contrasting fortunes for its two launch vehicles.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space. This marks another step in the region's revitalization as a key hub for space launches.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station represents a significant step in private orbital infrastructure development.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. This marks a significant step toward establishing a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations swiftly to support future missions.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope, marking its first mission since 2021.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on industry news without specifying the content. No specific details about the news were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The art of hadrons
summary: The news headline 'The art of hadrons' suggests a creative or artistic interpretation of particle physics, likely related to the Large Hadron Collider (LHC) or hadron collider research. The accompanying image may depict a visual representation of hadrons or particle collisions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025 in Bremen, Germany. These events aim to inform industry about ESA's planned activities, priorities, and opportunities.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its potential to revolutionize robotic operations in space. The innovation is expected to enhance efficiency and capabilities for future space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. Young researchers contribute innovative ideas to this process.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive component in the space industry, though specific details are not provided.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for beaming power to satellites in orbit. The funding will advance their mission to enable in-space power transmission.

title: US Government Studying Offshore Launch Opportunities
summary: The US Government is exploring offshore launch opportunities by studying the feasibility of building or repurposing infrastructure on the Outer Continental Shelf for space launch and reentry operations. The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) issued a Request for Information (RFI) to gather relevant data.

title: NATO Allies Plan to Stitch Together Sovereign Space Fleets
summary: Eight NATO allies are planning to integrate their national defense satellites into a unified network. This initiative aims to enhance collective space-based defense capabilities.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
summary: Blue Origin is seeking $10 billion in funding at a $130 billion valuation, marking a shift away from Jeff Bezos' sole financial support. The company is reportedly moving toward a more independent financial structure.

title: Auxilium Demos Tech To Enable Sustainable Bioprinting in Orbit
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies successfully created multiple types of organ tissues aboard the ISS, demonstrating scalable bioprinting technology for future orbital applications.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: ispace has secured 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for a lunar mission launching no earlier than 2030. This initiative marks the company's entry into the lunar integrator service market.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B to Scale RDRE Production
summary: Venus Aerospace has raised $91 million in a Series B funding round to scale production of its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine (RDRE) technology. The investment highlights growing industry interest in RDRE, a more efficient rocket propulsion method.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to vote on the Space Modernization Order on July 22, aiming to reform satellite licensing processes. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced the upcoming decision, which could modernize regulations for the space industry.

title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission launched 81 payloads to low Earth orbit (LEO), including the first commercial nuclear satellite. The mission lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base at 3:12 AM.

title: NASA Releases CLD Draft RFP
summary: NASA has released a draft Request for Proposal (RFP) for its Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) program, aiming to support stand-alone replacements to the International Space Station (ISS). This move follows industry progress in developing human habitation capabilities in low Earth orbit (LEO).

title: FCC Approves Reflect Orbital Demo Satellite to Test Redirecting Sunlight
summary: The FCC has approved Reflect Orbital's demonstration satellite to test redirecting sunlight back to Earth using a solar reflector. This marks a significant step in the company's efforts to explore space-based solar power technology.

title: Space42 and Skylo Test D2D Test on Thuraya-4
summary: Space42 and Skylo successfully tested direct-to-device messaging on the Thuraya-4 satellite, demonstrating SMS and SOS capabilities. The partners announced the achievement on July 9 and plan to expand the service.

title: Ovzon’s GEO Connectivity and Terminals Complete Arctic Tests
summary: Ovzon has successfully completed a series of Arctic tests to validate its GEO connectivity and terminal technology in harsh environments. The company conducted three High North expeditions to demonstrate the reliability of its systems.

title: Simera Sense to Supply Imagers for Canary Islands Satellite Constellation
summary: Simera Sense will supply multispectral imagers for the Canary Islands satellite constellation project. Telespazio Ibérica has selected Simera Sense as the supplier.

title: MDA Space Vertically Integrates in EO Analytics With Deal to Acquire Majority of CLS
summary: MDA Space is acquiring a majority stake in CLS, a French Earth observation data analytics company, to strengthen its vertical integration in the EO (Earth Observation) sector. This deal enhances MDA Space's capabilities in providing end-to-end solutions for Earth observation analytics.

title: SpaceX Files for 100,000-Satellite Gen-3 Constellation
summary: SpaceX has filed a request with the FCC to launch a third-generation satellite constellation consisting of 100,000 satellites aimed at providing multi-gigabit connectivity. The filing includes plans to utilize W- and D-band frequencies.

title: Telesat to Provide Mil-Ka Arctic Connectivity for Canada’s ESCP-P Program
summary: Telesat has secured a deal with the Canadian government to supply Military Ka-band satellite capacity for Canada's Enhanced Satellite Communications Project – Polar (ESCP-P) program. The agreement aims to enhance Arctic connectivity for military and government use.

title: Rivada Signs Connectivity Partner in Nepal
summary: Rivada Space Networks has partnered with a telecoms company in Nepal to provide connectivity services. The deal involves Mercantile Space, a communications technology firm in the country.

title: Impulse Space and Relativity Space Join the Field for Space Force NSSL Phase 3, Lane 1
summary: Impulse Space and Relativity Space have joined the competition for the U.S. Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3, Lane 1 awards. This expands the eligible field to seven companies.

title: Beyond Gravity Signs Gamechanging Contract with Ariane Group
summary: Beyond Gravity has signed a major contract with ArianeGroup, reinforcing its role in the European launch supply chain. The agreement is one of the largest in Beyond Gravity's history.
